evals.name: ts-shipping-switch-to-strategy
evals.prompt:
can you refactor this? I know the switch is ugly but I can't figure out the cleanest way out of it. it's in my checkout service and every time we add a new shipping provider I have to touch this plus like 3 other places — so I want something where adding a carrier is a one-file change. heres the code:

[attached: shipping.ts]
expected_behaviors: Replaces switch on provider with Strategy pattern or polymorphic provider objects
Carriers isolated so adding one is a one-file-local change (Open/Closed)
Domestic vs international rates modeled cleanly (not nested ifs per carrier)
Parameter object for the shipping request (Long Parameter List)
Magic numbers (markup rates, weight surcharge thresholds) promoted to named constants
No TS 'any', 'prefer-const' respected, readable output
Header comment lists applied refactorings, no essay

evals.name: py-order-processor-nested-conditionals
evals.prompt:
this is a legacy function in our order processing system. it works but it's a mess and we keep having bugs in it. refactor please — we're moving to python 3.11 so use whatever modern features make sense.

[attached: order_processor.py]
expected_behaviors: Guard clauses replace the stacked validation if-nots
Dataclasses introduced for Item, Order, PaymentInfo, etc. (Data Clumps, Primitive Obsession)
Promo codes become Strategy-style (or an enum + dispatch dict) — not an if-elif chain
Payment processor detection extracted and no longer uses string-prefix comparisons as control flow
Magic numbers (tax rate, free-shipping threshold, sale discount) named
Type hints on public functions
Error returns look like exceptions or a Result type — not stringly-typed error dicts mixed with success dicts
Header comment names the refactorings

evals.name: react-bloated-profile-card
evals.prompt:
my coworker wrote this react component and it's huge. can you clean it up? we use this for displaying user profile cards in our directory. we can add more files if it makes sense.

[attached: UserProfileCard.jsx]
expected_behaviors: Data fetching extracted to custom hooks (useUser, useUserPosts, or similar)
Display subcomponents extracted (ViewMode, EditForm, PostsList, Badges)
Edit form state collapsed (not two separate useStates when one object works)
Rules-of-hooks and exhaustive-deps respected
Error/loading/empty branches use guard returns cleanly
Per-file header comment listing the refactorings
Ambitious — multiple output files, not one file with inline everything
